rationale: The cited work is topically close to the citing study’s setting and outcome: both focus on prerelease movie signals and box-office prediction, and the cited note reports incremental predictive value of WOM spikiness beyond standard controls. That makes it potentially more than a generic mention (e.g., a non-neural benchmark or conceptual prior), but the evidence provided is only a single title-level reference match with no in-text citation context. With this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop nor clearly justified to keep.
